name: Richard Wyands
abstract: Richard Francis Wyands (July 2, 1928 – September 25, 2019) was an American jazz pianist, composer, and arranger, best known for his work as a side-man.

interviews.title: Richard Wyands interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 7, 2002

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Benny Carter and Illinois Jacquet

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: So we got Richard Wyands, who is – we made a record... Richard, I had known since when I was with Dinah Washington... Since then, that’s what the band has been.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Cobb — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Earl Watkins
about_quotes.evidence: Richard Wyands on piano, Vernon on bass, I was on drums, Flip, Jerome and Alan. The first time they had a trombonist.
about_quotes.doc_title: Earl Watkins interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Eiji Kitamura
about_quotes.evidence: And he brought his trio and joined my clarinet and a tour in Japan.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eiji Kitamura interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 13, 1999
